title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context
authors: Zihang Dai, Zhilin Yang, Yiming Yang, Jaime Carbonell et al.
hook: Imagine a language model that remembers what happened many sentences ago.
promise: You will understand how Transformer-XL enables language models to remember and reason beyond fixed-length context windows.
prerequisite_note: Bring an open mind about how computers process sequences, especially in language.

glossary.plain: The chunk of input a model can see at once when making predictions.
A mechanism that allows models to reuse information from previous segments.
Signals that help a model know the order of words in a sequence.
When a long sequence is split into pieces, breaking connections between them.
A measure of how well a model compresses or predicts text.
Passing hidden states from one chunk of data to the next, not just within the chunk.

cards.title: Let language models remember
body: Discover how Transformer-XL breaks the memory barrier and unlocks longer-range understanding in language models.

cards.title: How far can you remember?
body: Imagine trying to write a story, but you can only see the last paragraph, everything before is gone. That's how most language models work.
Transformer-XL was created to help models remember much further, so they can make sense of stories, articles, or code that stretch across many pages.

cards.title: From short-term to long-term memory
body: Traditional Transformers only see a fixed chunk of text, making it hard to connect ideas that are far apart.
Transformer-XL introduces a way to reuse information from previous chunks, letting the model build up memory over time.
cards.middle: Segment-level recurrence and new positional encoding
cards.caption: The paper's key move is linking chunks together so attention can span much further.

cards.title: How does context length change?
body: Toggle to see how models handle long documents with and without Transformer-XL's recurrence.
cards.offNote: Can only attend within a chunk. Loses track of earlier content. Context fragmentation splits up meaning.
cards.onNote: Remembers previous segments. Connects distant ideas. Reduces fragmentation and improves coherence.

cards.title: The recurrence trick
body: Transformer-XL passes hidden states from one segment to the next, almost like handing memory notes across time.
It also introduces a new positional encoding so that reused memories still make sense in their new context, keeping the sequence order unbroken.

cards.title: How much memory does your model have?
body: Slide to see what happens as you increase the context length a model can use.
cards.low: The model quickly forgets earlier information and struggles with long-range dependencies.
cards.mid: Some memory is carried over, so the model can link nearby ideas but may miss distant ones.
cards.high: The model connects events and ideas across very long stretches, generating more coherent and meaningful text.

cards.title: Which parts make Transformer-XL work?
body: Pick the ingredients that let Transformer-XL break the context barrier.

choices.label: Segment-level recurrence
choices.note: Lets the model reuse hidden states from earlier segments.

choices.label: Relative positional encoding
choices.note: Adapts positional signals so memory from previous segments fits smoothly.

choices.label: Efficient evaluation
choices.note: Speeds up inference by reusing computations across segments.

choices.label: Longer effective context
choices.note: Allows the model to attend over much longer text spans.

choices.label: Context fragmentation solution
choices.note: Prevents meaning from being lost at segment boundaries.

choices.label: Standard attention layers
choices.note: Still forms the backbone, but now enhanced by recurrence.

cards.title: How much better did Transformer-XL perform?
body: Transformer-XL smashed previous records for language modeling, handling much longer dependencies than RNNs or vanilla Transformers.
It achieved benchmark-leading results on several benchmarks and was dramatically faster at evaluation, making large-scale text modeling practical.
cards.caption: Transformer-XL set new records for both performance and efficiency in language modeling.

cards.title: Why Transformer-XL changed the game
body: By letting attention span across segments, Transformer-XL made it possible for models to understand and generate much longer, more coherent text.
Its innovations inspired later models that handle even bigger documents and more complex dependencies, shaping the future of language AI.

recap.label: Divide
recap.note: Split long text into manageable segments.

recap.label: Remember
recap.note: Pass hidden states across segments for memory.

recap.label: Align
recap.note: Use new positional encoding to keep order intact.

recap.label: Connect
recap.note: Attend over longer context for richer understanding.

cards.title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context
body: For the full technical details and results, see the original paper on arXiv.
